stem: A network performance study measures signal latency for 30 samples to determine if the process variability is below 50ms for 99% of packets. The sample standard deviation is 6.16 ms (sample variance = 37.93). Estimate the true population standard deviation of signal latency and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 6.16 ms. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [4.91, 8.28]. Point estimate: 6.16 ms.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (6.16) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
